original_jd_summary: An Early Childhood Educator position at a prestigious, boutique 40-place centre in Mulgrave. The role focuses on nature-based and Reggio Emilia pedagogy within an 'Exceeding' NQS rated environment.

company_context.mission_values: Nature-inspired, Reggio Emilia-influenced philosophy viewing children as competent researchers. Core values include advocacy, agency, curiosity, and a sense of wonder.
company_context.culture: Boutique, community-focused 'home-like' sanctuary. Collaborative and supportive 'founding team' environment that encourages professional bravery and curiosity in a close-knit setting.
recent_news: Opening a new Early Learning Centre in Hallam, Melbourne
Actively recruiting founding teams for strategic expansion in 2025-2026
Consistent 'Exceeding' NQS ratings across all seven quality areas
company_context.strategic_direction: Expansion of the 'boutique' childcare model while maintaining intimate service delivery and replicating 'Exceeding' NQS standards at new locations.

role_details.overview: A Permanent Part-Time (30 hours/week) position reporting to the Centre Director. The role acts as a curator of a 'home-like' sanctuary, implementing intentional pedagogy in a 40-place boutique environment.
key_responsibilities: Implement EYLF and VEYLDF based on Reggio Emilia principles
Document learning journeys via Storypark with a focus on 'intentional pedagogy'
Curate inquiry-based learning in expansive outdoor nature gardens and mud kitchens
Promote child agency and advocacy in daily interactions
Foster high-touch parent partnerships and community engagement
role_details.success_metrics: Maintenance of 'Exceeding' NQS quality standards, timely and high-quality Storypark documentation, and successful integration of nature-based learning outcomes.

required_qualifications: Certificate III in Early Childhood Education and Care (minimum)
Current Victorian Working with Children Check (Employee)
HLTAID012 First Aid, CPR, Asthma, and Anaphylaxis qualifications
Verified right to work in Australia
preferred_qualifications: Diploma of Early Childhood Education and Care
Practical experience with Storypark or similar digital platforms
Demonstrated knowledge of Reggio Emilia educational philosophy
Experience/Interest in nature-based or outdoor intentional pedagogy
cultural_fit_attributes: Collaborative mindset for a small, close-knit team
Commitment to being a 'professional advocate' for children

company_strengths: Prestigious 'Exceeding' NQS rating across all 7 areas
Boutique scale (40 places) allowing for deep, trusting relationships
Expansive, curriculum-central nature gardens and mud kitchens
what_you_get.growth_opportunities: Involvement in new centre openings and the chance to shape founding teams as the organization expands into 2026.
challenges_to_tackle: Sustaining high-touch service standards amidst sector-wide workforce shortages
Maintaining administrative excellence required for 'Exceeding' status

interview_preparation.likely_focus_areas: Practical application of Reggio Emilia philosophy, utilizing outdoor environments for intentional teaching, and building parent-partnerships in a boutique setting.
